footer.seo_heading: vCard QR Code Generator for Business Cards
footer.seo_text: Turn your contact details into a scannable QR code. Anyone who scans it can save your name, phone number, email, and more directly to their phone – no app needed. Great for business cards, email signatures, and capturing contact data via QR code.
footer.how_text_after: – an industry-standard format (vCard 3.0) that both iPhones and Android phones understand. When someone scans the QR code with their camera, it prompts them to save your contact information. No special app required.
footer.use_case_business_cards_text: Create a QR code for your business card so customers can save your contact details directly to their phone.
footer.use_case_free_generator_text: The static vCard QR Code Generator is free, works without signup, and exports PNG, SVG, or VCF files.
footer.use_case_contact_capture_text: Use the QR code for contact data capture in everyday workflows: scan, review the contact, and save it to the address book.
footer.about_text: An open-source QR code generator for business cards and contact info. Your data never leaves your browser – everything happens right on your device.
footer.privacy_1: Everything happens on your device – nothing is sent anywhere
footer.privacy_2: No account needed, nothing is saved or stored
footer.privacy_3: No tracking, no ads, no data collection of any kind

seo.title_business_card_guide: Create QR Code for Business Card – vCard QR Guide

vcard_page.heading: What is a vCard?
vcard_page.intro: A vCard is a standardized file format (.vcf) for sharing contact information. It contains details like name, phone number, email address, company, and more – all in a small text file.
vcard_page.usage_heading: What is a vCard used for?
vcard_page.usage_text: vCards are used to share contact information easily and without errors. Instead of typing in a phone number, you can transfer the data directly to your address book via QR code or file.
vcard_page.scan_iphone: iPhone: Open the Camera app and point it at the QR code. A notification will appear to save the contact.
vcard_page.scan_android: Android: Most camera apps detect QR codes automatically. Alternatively, you can use Google Lens.
vcard_page.print_heading: Printing QR codes on business cards
vcard_page.print_text: Download the QR code as PNG (1024×1024 pixels) or SVG (vector graphic). For printing, we recommend a minimum size of 2×2 cm and SVG format for the best quality.
vcard_page.format_text: This tool uses vCard 3.0 – the most widely supported and compatible format. It works with all common smartphones and contact apps.

guide_page.heading: Create QR code for business card
guide_page.intro: Create a vCard QR code for your business card so contact details can be saved directly on iPhone or Android after scanning – no app, no typing.
guide_page.benefit_fast_text: Enter contact details, check the QR code, and download the file.
guide_page.benefit_print_text: SVG for business cards, flyers, and print files; PNG for digital use.
guide_page.benefit_private_text: The static QR code is generated locally. Your contact details do not leave the browser.
guide_page.how_heading: How a vCard QR code works on a business card
guide_page.how_text: A vCard QR code stores contact details in a smartphone-compatible format. After scanning, the phone offers to save the contact directly to the address book.
guide_page.step_1_text: Add name, company, phone, email, website, and optionally address or social profiles.
guide_page.step_2_text: Scan it with your phone and check whether the contact is saved exactly as intended.
guide_page.step_3_text: Use SVG for business cards and place the code large enough to scan reliably.
guide_page.contact_heading: Which contact details belong in the QR code?
guide_page.contact_text: For business cards, fewer clear fields are better than an overloaded QR code. The more content you add, the denser the code becomes.
guide_page.contact_name: Name and optionally title or role
guide_page.contact_phone: Phone number, preferably with international prefix
guide_page.contact_email: Email address that will remain valid
guide_page.contact_company: Company, job title, and business address if relevant
guide_page.contact_website: Website or profile with more information
guide_page.print_heading: SVG, PNG, or VCF: which format should you use?
guide_page.print_text: For printed business cards, sharp edges and reliable contrast matter. Always test the QR code at the final printed size.
guide_page.format_svg_text: Best for business cards because the vector graphic stays sharp at every size.
guide_page.format_png_text: Good for email signatures, websites, or previews. The 1024×1024 file also works for many simple print cases.
guide_page.format_vcf_text: The VCF file contains the same contact details and can also be sent or saved directly.
guide_page.static_text: A static vCard QR code stores the contact details directly in the code. It works without a server and is very private, but cannot be changed after printing. If you need editable details later, use a dynamic QR code with a URL.
guide_page.scan_text: On iPhone, the Camera app is enough. Most Android camera apps also detect QR codes directly; Google Lens works as a fallback. After scanning, the phone offers to save the contact.
guide_page.ready_text: Create the QR code, test it on your phone, and download it for your print files.
guide_page.q1: How large should the QR code be on a business card?
guide_page.a1: As a rule of thumb, 2.5 × 2.5 cm works well. If you include many contact details or use low contrast, make it larger.
guide_page.q2: Can I print the QR code in color?
guide_page.a2: Yes, as long as contrast stays high. A dark code on a light background is the most reliable choice.
guide_page.q3: Does the person scanning need a special app?
guide_page.a3: No. iPhone and most Android phones recognize vCard QR codes through the camera or Google Lens.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: A vCard can contain name, phone numbers, email addresses, company, job title, address, website, and social profiles. All fields are optional – you can include exactly what you want to share.
faq.q2: How big should I print the QR code?
faq.a2: For reliable scanning, we recommend a minimum size of 2×2 cm. For business cards, 2.5×2.5 cm usually works well. The more data in the QR code, the larger it should be printed.
faq.q3: Which file format should I use?
faq.a3: For printing, we recommend SVG (vector graphic) – it can be printed at any size without loss of quality. PNG works well for digital use (email signatures, websites).
faq.q4: Does the QR code work on all phones?
faq.a4: Yes. vCard QR codes work on iPhones and Android devices. The camera app recognizes the QR code and offers to save the contact.
faq.a5: No. Everything happens directly in your browser. No data is sent to a server, stored, or shared.
faq.q6: Can I customize the QR code colors?
faq.a6: Yes. You can customize the QR code color and background. Just make sure there is enough contrast so the code remains easy to scan.
faq.q7: How do you capture contact details with a QR code?
faq.a7: You create a vCard QR code here with your contact details. Anyone who scans the code with their phone camera gets the option to save the contact right away – no typing, no form. That’s how contact data capture via QR code works: one scan, contact in the address book.
faq.q8: Can I create a QR code for my business card for free?
faq.a8: Yes. The static QR code for business cards is free and works without an account. You can download it as PNG or SVG and use it on printed business cards, flyers, or email signatures.
faq.q9: What is the difference between QR code, vCard, and VCF?
faq.a9: The vCard is the contact format, VCF is the file extension, and the QR code is the scannable representation of it. This tool generates a vCard QR code that phones recognize as a contact.
faq.q10: Can I change the QR code after printing?
faq.a10: A static vCard QR code stores the contact details directly in the code and cannot be changed after printing. For editable contact details after printing, use a dynamic QR code.
